skill.name: ui-visual-validator
skill.description: Rigorous visual validation expert specializing in UI testing, design system compliance, and accessibility verification. Masters screenshot analysis, visual regression testing, and component validation. Use PROACTIVELY to verify UI modifications have achieved their intended goals through comprehensive visual analysis.
skill.summary: Guides rigorous screenshot-based UI review, visual regression checks, design system validation, and accessibility assessment.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported one external-command pattern and multiple weak-cryptography patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown prose. No executable scripts, network calls, cryptographic code, prompt injection attempts, or data exfiltration behavior were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Analyzer Match: Inline Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md:27. The line is a Markdown inline document reference and does not execute a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a Markdown path wrapped in backticks, not a shell, Ruby, or script context. No executable code path is present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Analyzer Match: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by ordinary UI validation and verification language. SKILL.md contains guidance text only and no cryptographic algorithm, hash implementation, or secret handling logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are descriptive Markdown about visual checks, accessibility validation, and design verification. No crypto API, weak algorithm name, executable code, or secret material appears in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: UI changes can look correct in code while failing in real screens. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through rigorous screenshot review, accessibility checks, and design system validation.
actual_capabilities: Reviews screenshots for visible differences, layout issues, and incomplete UI changes.
Checks design system alignment for colors, typography, spacing, icons, and component states.
Evaluates visual accessibility concerns, including contrast, focus indicators, and readable hierarchy.
Guides responsive validation across mobile, tablet, desktop, and browser breakpoints.
Supports visual regression workflows using tools such as Playwright, Storybook, Percy, Chromatic, and BackstopJS.
Produces clear pass, partial pass, or fail assessments with remediation recommendations.
limitations: It depends on screenshots, rendered pages, or clear visual evidence supplied by the user.
It cannot prove runtime behavior that is not visible in the reviewed evidence.
It does not replace automated accessibility testing or manual assistive technology testing.
It can recommend visual testing tools, but it does not include executable test scripts.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Request Screenshots
use_cases.description: Compare before and after screenshots to decide whether a UI change met its stated goal.

use_cases.title: Validate Design System Adoption
use_cases.description: Check whether components follow approved spacing, typography, color, state, and icon patterns.

use_cases.title: Audit Visual Accessibility
use_cases.description: Assess focus visibility, contrast, text scaling, layout hierarchy, and error state clarity.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Screenshot Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation of a single screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this screenshot against the stated UI goal. Start with direct observations, then state whether the goal is achieved, partially achieved, or not achieved.

prompt_templates.title: Responsive Layout Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these mobile, tablet, and desktop screenshots. Identify layout, spacing, hierarchy, and interaction state issues at each breakpoint.

prompt_templates.title: Design System Compliance Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these component screenshots for design system compliance. Check typography, color tokens, spacing, icons, states, and accessibility concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Visual Regression Gate
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release or pull request decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate these before and after screenshots as a release gate. List confirmed changes, unexpected regressions, accessibility risks, and required fixes before approval.

output_examples.input: Validate whether the redesigned primary button meets the accessibility and design goals.
output_examples.output: From the visual evidence, the button has clearer hierarchy and consistent spacing. The contrast appears acceptable, but the focus state is not shown, so keyboard accessibility cannot be confirmed.

output_examples.input: Compare mobile and desktop screenshots for the new navigation.
output: Desktop navigation keeps stable spacing and visible active state.
Mobile navigation collapses, but the menu icon has weak contrast.
The change is partially achieved because one breakpoint needs visual correction.

output_examples.input: Assess whether the modal overlay blocks background interaction visually.
output_examples.output: From the visual evidence, the overlay dims the page and centers the modal. Background buttons remain visually prominent, so the interaction boundary should be strengthened.

best_practices: Provide before and after screenshots with the exact UI goal.
Include key states such as hover, focus, loading, error, empty, and disabled.
Validate across representative breakpoints, themes, and browser targets.
anti_patterns: Do not claim success from code changes without rendered visual evidence.
Do not review only the happy path when error and loading states changed.
Do not treat any visible difference as proof that the intended goal was met.

faq.question: What evidence should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide screenshots, the intended UI goal, target breakpoints, and any relevant design system rules.

faq.question: Can this replace visual regression tooling?
faq.answer: No. It helps interpret visual evidence and plan checks, but automated tooling is still useful for repeatable regression coverage.

faq.question: Does it check accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides visual checks for contrast, focus indicators, readability, hierarchy, and visual feedback.

faq.question: Can it review responsive designs?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare screenshots across mobile, tablet, desktop, and other supplied viewport sizes.

faq.question: Does it execute tests?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides review methodology and recommendations, but it does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Which tools does it reference?
faq.answer: It references common visual testing tools, including Playwright, Storybook, Chromatic, Percy, Applitools, BackstopJS, Cypress, and Jest Image Snapshot.
